title: ffi: save_identity_to_file writes key material to world-readable tmp file before rename — key exposed on Linux
description: ffi/src/api/identity.rs save_identity_to_file() writes the 64-byte raw identity secret to a .tmp file with fs::write() (line 68), which uses the process umask. On Linux with default umask 022, the tmp file is created mode 0644 — world-readable. The rename() at line 69 preserves the file permissions. The code comment (lines 52-55) acknowledges this but frames it as an Android-only concern. On a Linux system (or any Unix with a permissive umask), another process running as the same user, or root, can read the identity secret from the tmp file (before rename) or from the final file (after rename). The fix is to open the tmp file with O_CREAT|O_EXCL|0600 permissions before writing. nie-ea6a.2 tracks the monero.key case; this is the ffi identity file case.
